summary: Iter 9 â€” verified Live-page refactor: empty state, last-match persistence, continue flow, entries from Time Machine + Players â†’ Best Match all open the SPECIFIC clicked match. No hardcoded runtime match_ids or Rinku narration. Backend regressions (Kohli/Bumrah career ratings) intact.

ui_bugs.component: Players grid â€” RatingBadge inside player-card button
ui_bugs.issue: React hydration warning: <button> cannot be a descendant of <button> (RatingBadge renders a button inside the player-card button)

issues: Recharts warns width(-1)/height(-1) on first render before container measures â€” cosmetic, chart still renders correctly after data arrives

action_items: (Optional/low) Wrap RatingBadge with a non-button wrapper (or disable interactive when inside another button) to remove nested <button> hydration warning on /players.
(Optional) Set explicit min-height on MomentumChart's ResponsiveContainer to silence Recharts width/height warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Live.jsx correctly branches on requestedMatchId and calls saveLastMatch on successful load â€” no featured fallback remains.
LiveEmptyState conditionally renders continue-last-match only when preview exists; testids match spec.
lastMatch.js key 'pitchwise:lastMatch:v1' persists match_id, teams, team_short, season, date, venue, curation_title, result_summary â€” verified live.
stream.py narration no longer contains 'Rinku' or over==19 hardcoded branch. Runtime narration is generic (SIX at Ox.y / pressure ...).
Note: backend/data/ingest_cricsheet.py still references match_id '1359487' and 'Rinku's 5 Sixes' but this is legitimate curation metadata (per problem statement, not a bug).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (7/7 flows: empty first-time, empty returning, persistence, continue, time-machine open, time-machine no-leak, playersâ†’bestâ†’live)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Live page correctly shows LiveEmptyState when no ?match_id. localStorage key is 'pitchwise:lastMatch:v1'. Match cards on TimeMachine use data-testid='match-card-<match_id>'. Players page uses 'player-card-<id>'; PlayerProfile best/recent rows use 'best-<match_id>' and 'recent-<match_id>' (careful â€” 'best-performances' and 'recent-matches' section wrappers also start with these prefixes, so scope selectors inside those sections).
